heading: Relief for airports
label: 9121 Relief for airports

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated for fiscal year 2021, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $8,000,000,000, to remain available until , for assistance to sponsors of airports, as such terms are defined in , to be made available to prevent, prepare for, and respond to coronavirus.September 30, 2024section 47102 of title 49

children.label: A
children.text: may not be used for any purpose not directly related to the airport; and

children.label: B
children.text: may not be provided to any airport that was allocated in excess of 4 years of operating funds to prevent, prepare for, and respond to coronavirus in fiscal year 2020.

children.heading: Operating expenses and debt service payments
children.label: 1 Operating expenses and debt service payments

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not more than $6,492,000,000 shall be made available for primary airports, as such term is defined in , and certain cargo airports, for costs related to operations, personnel, cleaning, sanitization, janitorial services, combating the spread of pathogens at the airport, and debt service payments.section 47102 of title 49

children.label: i
children.text: shall not be subject to the reduced apportionments under ;section 47114(f) of title 49

children.label: ii
children.text: shall first be apportioned as set forth in sections 47114(c)(1)(A), 47114(c)(1)(C)(i), 47114(c)(1)(C)(ii), 47114(c)(2)(A), 47114(c)(2)(B), and 47114(c)(2)(E) of title 49; and111See References in Text note below.1

children.label: iii
children.text: shall not be subject to a maximum apportionment limit set forth in .section 47114(c)(1)(B) of title 49

children.heading: Remaining amounts
children.label: C Remaining amounts
children.text: Any amount remaining after distribution under subparagraph (B) shall be distributed to the sponsor of each primary airport (as such term is defined in ) based on each such primary airport’s passenger enplanements compared to the total passenger enplanements of all such primary airports in calendar year 2019.section 47102 of title 49

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not more than $608,000,000 allocated under subsection (a)(1) shall be available to pay a Federal share of 100 percent of the costs for any grant awarded in fiscal year 2021, or in fiscal year 2020 with less than a 100-percent Federal share, for an airport development project (as such term is defined in ).section 47102 of title 49

children.heading: Remaining amounts
children.label: B Remaining amounts
children.text: Any amount remaining under this paragraph shall be distributed as described in paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not more than $100,000,000 shall be made available for general aviation and commercial service airports that are not primary airports (as such terms are defined in ) for costs related to operations, personnel, cleaning, sanitization, janitorial services, combating the spread of pathogens at the airport, and debt service payments.section 47102 of title 49

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: B Distribution
children.text: Amounts made available under this paragraph shall be apportioned to each non-primary airport based on the categories published in the most current National Plan of Integrated Airport Systems, reflecting the percentage of the aggregate published eligible development costs for each such category, and then dividing the allocated funds evenly among the eligible airports in each category, rounding up to the nearest thousand dollars.

children.heading: Remaining amounts
children.label: C Remaining amounts
children.text: Any amount remaining under this paragraph shall be distributed as described in paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not more than $800,000,000 shall be made available for sponsors of primary airports to provide relief from rent and minimum annual guarantees to airport concessions, of which at least $640,000,000 shall be available to provide relief to eligible small airport concessions and of which at least $160,000,000 shall be available to provide relief to eligible large airport concessions located at primary airports.

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: B Distribution
children.text: The amounts made available for each set-aside in this paragraph shall be distributed to the sponsor of each primary airport (as such term is defined in ) based on each such primary airport’s passenger enplanements compared to the total passenger enplanements of all such primary airports in calendar year 2019.section 47102 of title 49

children.label: i
children.text: the sponsor shall provide such relief from , until the sponsor has provided relief equaling the total grant amount, to the extent practicable and to the extent permissible under State laws, local laws, and applicable trust indentures; andMarch 21, 2021

children.label: ii
children.text: for each set-aside, the sponsor shall provide relief from rent and minimum annual guarantee obligations to each eligible airport concession in an amount that reflects each eligible airport concession’s proportional share of the total amount of the rent and minimum annual guarantees of those eligible airport concessions at such airport.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 1 Administrative expenses
children.text: The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration may retain up to 0.1 percent of the funds provided under this section to fund the award of, and oversight by the Administrator of, grants made under this section.

children.heading: Required retention
children.label: A Required retention
children.text: As a condition for receiving funds provided under this section, an airport shall continue to employ, through , at least 90 percent of the number of individuals employed (after making adjustments for retirements or voluntary employee separations) by the airport as of .September 30, 2021March 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: the airport is experiencing economic hardship as a direct result of the requirement; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirement reduces aviation safety or security.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: The workforce retention requirement shall not apply to nonhub airports or nonprimary airports receiving funds under this section.

children.heading: Noncompliance
children.label: D Noncompliance
children.text: Any financial assistance provided under this section to an airport that fails to comply with the workforce retention requirement described in subparagraph (A), and does not otherwise qualify for a waiver or exception under this paragraph, shall be subject to clawback by the Secretary.

children.heading: Eligible large airport concession
children.label: 1 Eligible large airport concession
children.text: The term “eligible large airport concession” means a concession (as defined in section 23.3 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations), that is in-terminal and has maximum gross receipts, averaged over the previous three fiscal years, of more than $56,420,000.

children.label: A
children.text: a small business with maximum gross receipts, averaged over the previous 3 fiscal years, of less than $56,420,000; or

children.label: B
children.text: is a joint venture (as defined in section 23.3 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations).
